q: The sea breeze is a laminar flow.
Fluid is liquid only.
The density of the liquid is constant for any pressure at a constant temperature.

The internal friction in the fluids is called viscosity.
In an ideal laminar flow tube, the flow speed is always constant.
The studying of liquid at rest is called hydrodynamics.
For a flying plane, the air pressure is higher above the wing than below the wing.
Gases are compressible.

During cyclones, moving air gains speed above the roof of a house.
The volume of a liquid varies with pressure at a constant temperature.
Most of the fluids are ideal ones.
Fluid is a substance that offers permanent resistance to deforming forces.
Viscosity can be thought of as a measure of a fluid's thickness.
In a turbulent flow, each layer of the fluid moves smoothly past the adjacent layers separately.
Cohesion refers to forces between molecules of different substances.
The smaller the area of the pipe, the faster the speed of the fluid.
The viscosity of liquids increases rapidly with an increase in temperature.
Adhesion refers to forces between molecules of the same substance.

For ideal laminar fluid flow, the fluid flow has friction.
The volume of a gas depends on the temperature.

Capillary action is the rise or depression of a liquid in a narrow tube.

If the flow or the path of the fluid particles is irregular, it is a laminar flow.

The fluid dynamics concerns the fluid in motion.

Viscosity is an extensive property.
The work done is equal to the change in total mechanical energy.
The larger the area of the pipe, the slower the speed of the fluid.

In turbulent flow, the streamlines do not cross each other.
The fluid statics concentrates on the fluids in motion.
Capillarity does not occur for non-wetting liquids.
The study of liquid at rest is called hydrostatics.
For ideal laminar fluid flow, the friction may be neglected.
During cyclones, the pressure on the roof is higher than the pressure inside the house.
For ideal laminar fluid flow, the fluid is incompressible.

The surface tension is a concept of hydrodynamics.
Fluid is either liquid or gas.

In laminar flow, the velocity of the fluid at a point is continuously changing.
Mechanical energy is the sum of potential energy and kinetic energy.
If the flow or the path of the fluid particles is always whirling, it is turbulent flow.
The volume of a gas depends on the pressure.
